articles.title: Todd Blanche faces high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general
articles.summary: Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he faces a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.
articles.titleZh: 托德·布兰奇面临司法部长的高风险确认听证会
articles.summaryZh: 布兰奇在特朗普总统第二任期初期就获得参议院批准，担任司法部二号官员。现在，他在寻求最高职位时面临着一场有争议的听证会。

contentParagraphs.en: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche listens during a news conference at the Justice Department building on July 1.
contentParagraphs.zh: 7 月 1 日，代理司法部长托德·布兰奇在司法部大楼举行的新闻发布会上聆听。

contentParagraphs.en: Todd Blanche, President Trump's former personal lawyer and his pick for attorney general, is set to appear for what could be a contentious confirmation hearing Wednesday amid questions about his handling of the Epstein files, the targeting of Trump's political foes and the broader politicization of justice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普总统的前私人律师、他任命的司法部长托德·布兰奇(Todd Blanche)将于周三出席一场可能引发争议的确认听证会，其间，人们对他处理爱泼斯坦档案、针对特朗普政敌以及更广泛的司法政治化等问题提出质疑。

contentParagraphs.en: Blanche previously won Senate confirmation early in Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department, but it is unclear whether he can muster enough support this time to be confirmed for the top job.
contentParagraphs.zh: 布兰奇此前曾在特朗普第二任期初期获得参议院确认，出任司法部二号官员，但目前尚不清楚他这次能否获得足够的支持来获得最高职位的确认。

contentParagraphs.en: The confirmation hearing is set to start at 9 a.m. ET. Watch it live:
contentParagraphs.zh: 确认听证会定于东部时间上午 9 点开始。观看直播：

contentParagraphs.en: Democrats appear unified in opposition to his nomination. That leaves Blanche's fate in the hands of a small number of Republicans who have voiced concerns about some of his actions at the department and could torpedo his nomination in the Senate, where the Republican Party has a razor-thin majority.
contentParagraphs.zh: 民主党人似乎一致反对他的提名。这使得布兰奇的命运掌握在少数共和党人手中，他们对他在司法部的一些行为表示担忧，并可能破坏他在参议院的提名，而共和党在参议院拥有微弱多数。

contentParagraphs.en: Republican concerns largely center on Blanche's involvement in the creation of a nearly $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund as part of a settlement with Trump to end his lawsuit against the IRS over his leaked tax returns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党的担忧主要集中在布兰奇参与设立近18亿美元的反武器化基金，作为与特朗普达成和解的一部分，以结束他因泄露纳税申报单而对国税局提起的诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: After bipartisan pushback, Blanche said the fund isn't moving forward — but the part of the settlement that shields Trump, his family and his businesses from IRS audits of past tax returns remains in place.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在两党的抵制后，布兰奇表示，该基金不会向前推进，但和解协议中保护特朗普、他的家人和他的企业免受国税局对过去纳税申报表审计的部分仍然存在。

contentParagraphs.en: On Monday, a federal judge blasted the Justice Department over the settlement, saying it was an attempt to "earmark billions of dollars from American taxpayers to redress grievances not defined in the law."
contentParagraphs.zh: 周一，一名联邦法官对司法部的和解协议进行了猛烈抨击，称这是试图“从美国纳税人那里拨出数十亿美元来纠正法律中未定义的不满”。

contentParagraphs.en: Sens. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., and John Cornyn, R-Texas, are among the handful of Republican lawmakers to raise questions about the settlement. They are both members of the Senate Judiciary Committee, where Blanche will appear on Wednesday for his confirmation hearing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 北卡罗来纳州共和党参议员托姆·蒂利斯 (Thom Tillis) 和德克萨斯州共和党参议员约翰·科宁 (John Cornyn) 是少数对和解协议提出质疑的共和党议员之一。他们都是参议院司法委员会的成员，布兰奇将于周三出席该委员会的确认听证会。

contentParagraphs.en: A single Republican "no" vote on the committee could kill Blanche's nomination.
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党在该委员会中投反对票就可能会扼杀布兰奇的提名。

contentParagraphs.en: Targeting of Trump's political foes
contentParagraphs.zh: 针对特朗普的政治敌人

contentParagraphs.en: Blanche has led the Justice Department since April, when Trump pushed out his first attorney general, Pam Bondi, and elevated Blanche to the top job on an acting basis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自四月以来，布兰奇一直领导司法部，当时特朗普罢免了他的第一任司法部长帕姆·邦迪，并以代理身份将布兰奇提升为最高职位。

contentParagraphs.en: Under Blanche's leadership, the department has aggressively pursued Trump's promised campaign of revenge against his perceived political enemies. The department announced indictments against former FBI Director James Comey as well as the Southern Poverty Law Center, the storied civil rights group long criticized by conservatives.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在布兰奇的领导下，该部门积极推行特朗普承诺的对其视为政敌的报复行动。该部门宣布对前联邦调查局局长詹姆斯·科米以及长期受到保守派批评的著名民权组织南方贫困法律中心提起起诉。

contentParagraphs.en: The department also opened an investigation that centers on lawsuits that the writer E. Jean Carroll brought and won against Trump for sexual abuse and defamation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该部门还启动了一项调查，重点关注作家 E·让·卡罗尔 (E. Jean Carroll) 针对特朗普的性虐待和诽谤提起并胜诉的诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump has praised Blanche, saying in May that he's "doing a great job" leading the Justice Department.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普称赞布兰奇，五月表示他领导司法部“做得很好”。

contentParagraphs.en: The top Republican on the Senate Judiciary Committee, Iowa's Chuck Grassley, welcomed Blanche's nomination, calling him "well qualified" and saying he has "shown his dedication to restoring law and order across our country."
contentParagraphs.zh: 参议院司法委员会的共和党高层、爱荷华州的查克·格拉斯利对布兰奇的提名表示欢迎，称他“非常合格”，并表示他“表现出了恢复全国法律和秩序的奉献精神”。

contentParagraphs.en: The National Association of Police Organizations has also voiced support for Blanche's nomination, saying that "he has built a strong partnership with the law enforcement community."
contentParagraphs.zh: 全国警察组织协会也表示支持布兰奇的提名，称“他与执法界建立了牢固的伙伴关系”。

contentParagraphs.en: Democrats, in contrast, have accused Blanche of destroying the Justice Department's traditional independence from the White House, weaponizing its prosecutorial powers and undermining the rule of law.
contentParagraphs.zh: 相比之下，民主党人指责布兰奇破坏了司法部独立于白宫的传统，将其检察权武器化并破坏法治。

contentParagraphs.en: The top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, Dick Durbin of Illinois, said that when Blanche was under consideration for the No. 2 job last year, he reminded Blanche that maintaining the department's independence from politics and the White House was critical.
contentParagraphs.zh: 参议院司法委员会民主党领袖、伊利诺伊州的迪克·德宾表示，去年考虑布兰奇担任二号人物时，他提醒布兰奇，保持司法部独立于政治和白宫至关重要。

contentParagraphs.en: "Regrettably, over the past year, he has disregarded that call and erased that line, placing loyalty to President Trump above all else and damaging the independence and credibility of the department in ways that will have ramifications for decades," Durbin said last month.
contentParagraphs.zh: 杜宾上个月表示：“遗憾的是，在过去的一年里，他无视这一呼吁并删除了这条路线，将对特朗普总统的忠诚置于首位，并损害了该部门的独立性和信誉，这将产生数十年的影响。”

contentParagraphs.en: Accused of "improper, unlawful" firings
contentParagraphs.zh: 被指控“不当、非法”解雇

contentParagraphs.en: Many legal observers and department veterans have similar concerns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 许多法律观察家和部门资深人士也有类似的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: A letter signed by some 1,200 former Department of Justice employees urged the Senate to reject Blanche's nomination, saying he has attacked the apolitical career workforce by firing career attorneys, agents and analysts for "improper, unlawful reasons."
contentParagraphs.zh: 一封由大约 1,200 名前司法部雇员签署的信敦促参议院拒绝布兰奇的提名，称布兰奇以“不正当、非法的理由”解雇职业律师、代理人和分析师，攻击了非政治职业劳动力。

contentParagraphs.en: The effects of those firings, the letter says, will be felt across the United States.
contentParagraphs.zh: 信中称，这些解雇的影响将波及整个美国。

contentParagraphs.en: "They've meant that much of the department's vital work isn't being done, or isn't being done as well — leaving communities less safe, Americans' rights less protected, and our national security more vulnerable," says the letter, which was organized and sent by Justice Connection, a nonprofit that helps DOJ workers find legal and ethics help.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这封信说：“这意味着司法部的许多重要工作没有完成，或者没有完成——导致社区安全性降低，美国人的权利受到更少保护，我们的国家安全更加脆弱。”这封信是由正义连接组织和发送的。正义连接是一家帮助司法部工作人员寻求法律和道德帮助的非营利组织。

contentParagraphs.en: Blanche, for his part, has brushed aside the criticism.
contentParagraphs.zh: 布兰奇则对这些批评置之不理。

contentParagraphs.en: "There's 1,200 former DOJ employees, I think, out of what, 40,000?" he said in an interview with a local Alaska TV station. "I don't know. I'm not a math guy, but that's not a very high percentage."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我想，司法部有 1,200 名前雇员，其中有 40,000 名？”他在接受阿拉斯加当地电视台采访时说道。 “我不知道。我不是数学专家，但这并不是一个很高的比例。”

contentParagraphs.en: He said he was still going to work with Republicans and Democrats to answer any questions about his nomination.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他表示，他仍将与共和党和民主党合作，回答有关他提名的任何问题。

contentParagraphs.en: In a social media post on the eve of the confirmation hearing, Trump again lauded Blanche, saying: "He is a great lawyer, always very fair, and every Republican Senator should vote to CONFIRM Todd Blanche, ASAP!"
contentParagraphs.zh: 在确认听证会前夕的社交媒体帖子中，特朗普再次赞扬布兰奇，称：“他是一位伟大的律师，总是非常公平，每位共和党参议员都应该尽快投票支持托德·布兰奇！”

articles.title: Global cooperation needed to tackle AI threats, says Bank of England governor
articles.summary:
Andrew Bailey warns that US will not be able to achieve its ambitions alone
The Bank of England governor has called for international cooperation to tackle growing AI threats, warning that the US and Trump administration would not be able to achieve their ambitions alone.
Andrew
articles.titleZh: 英格兰银行行长表示，应对人工智能威胁需要全球合作
articles.summaryZh:
安德鲁·贝利警告说，美国无法独自实现其雄心
英国央行行长呼吁国际合作应对日益严重的人工智能威胁，并警告说，美国和特朗普政府无法单独实现其雄心。
安德鲁

contentParagraphs.en: The Bank of England governor has called for international cooperation to tackle growing AI threats, warning that the US and Trump administration would not be able to achieve their ambitions alone.Andrew Bailey’s comments come weeks after the US president, Donald Trump, temporarily banned foreigners from using Anthropic’s powerful Claude Mythos model.Speaking to the Guardian before a speech to financial bosses at the annual Mansion House dinner in London, Bailey said governments around the world must join forces to ensure bad actors did not get hold of powerful – and potentially destabilising – digital tools.“We’ve got to get better international understandings of how we deal with the introduction of frontier AI,” Bailey said, arguing this would require stronger coordinated testing to ensure AI models were safe to put into wider circulation.The Trump administration has already frustrated allies seeking a joint approach, having temporarily banned the American AI company Anthropic from letting foreigners use some of its most powerful models, including Claude Mythos, which experts have warned pose potential threats to cyber defences.While the ban was lifted just weeks later, Bailey said the US should be mindful that it may not be able to secure itself against growing cyber threats, or set up robust recovery plans, without global cooperation.“The US can’t achieve what it sensibly wants to achieve, in terms of strengthening defences, on its own because it is a highly interconnected system,” he said.He elaborated on that point in his speech to City bosses on Tuesday, saying: “No country can seal itself off from the cross-border nature of systems that are prevalent today.”Bailey’s call for action on AI came alongside a defiant Mansion House speech from the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, as she prepares to exit the Treasury. In what is likely to be one of her last set-piece appearances, Reeves defended her record and warned her successor not to squander the “platform of stability” she had created.“The record of the last two years makes clear that governments can achieve that [radical] change only when they combine radicalism with credibility,” she said.“I had to earn that credibility in opposition, and I have proven it in government in every action that I have taken since I became chancellor. That hard-won credibility must be sustained and the foundations maintained if this work … is to continue.”Andy Burnham is set to be confirmed as Labour leader on Friday, and to take over as prime minister next week. He is widely expected to appoint a new chancellor.Reeves cited a string of statistics that she said proved she had made the right choices in her two years in post.“Last year, borrowing fell from 5.2% to 4.2% of GDP – its lowest level in six years. Investment is up, productivity is up, and wages are up too. Waiting lists are falling faster than at any time in the last 17 years and half a million children will be lifted out of poverty during the course of this parliament,” she said.“If I had said two years ago that this is where we would be today, people would have doubted it, but we have proven the doubters wrong.”skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionAs the conflict in the Middle East again flared up, after the collapse of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, Reeves also warned that the UK economy would continue to be buffeted by shocks.“The resumption of hostilities in the Middle East in the last few days has shown that our economic resilience will continue to be tested, and the market response to those changes shows that there is still work to do to insure our economy and our country against a volatile global landscape,” she said.The interest rate, or yield, on 10-year UK government debt hit its highest level since May on Tuesday, above 5%, before falling slightly below later after Trump appeared to drop the threat of imposing a 20% levy on ships transiting the strait of Hormuz.A new chancellor would have to give 10 weeks’ notice to the independent Office for Budget Responsibility to produce a new forecast before a planned budget in the autumn.If sustained, higher gilt yields would feed through into the government’s cost of borrowing, eating into the £23.6bn of “headroom” Reeves set aside against her fiscal rules at the last forecast in March.Whoever is in charge at the Treasury will also have to find an additional £4.7bn over the next four years, to fund the defence investment plan.Burnham is believed to be keen to announce a cost of living package, with households predicted to be hit by higher energy bills this winter as the impact of the war takes its toll.Bailey’s international cooperation comments came as Demis Hassabis, the British Nobel laureate and entrepreneur behind Google Deepmind called for a US-led global AI watchdog, to help test advanced models and hit the brakes on their development if they pose too many risks.
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

contentParagraphs.en: The essay, posted on X on Tuesday, suggested that an AI model that had the same cognitive capabilities as the human brain, was “probably only a few short years away”.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这篇周二在X上发表的文章表明，一种与人类大脑具有相同认知能力的人工智能模型“可能只有短短几年的时间”。

articles.title: Inflation slowed sharply -- but it may not last
articles.summary: Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.
articles.titleZh: 通货膨胀急剧放缓，但可能不会持续
articles.summaryZh: 6 月份年度通胀率达到 3.5%，低于 5 月份的三年多高位，但随着能源成本再次飙升，与伊朗冲突的重新爆发可能会推高通胀。

contentParagraphs.en: Inflation slowed sharply — but it may not last
contentParagraphs.zh: 通货膨胀大幅放缓—但可能不会持续

contentParagraphs.en: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5893379/nx-s1-9848349" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">
contentParagraphs.zh: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5893379/nx-s1-9848349" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">

contentParagraphs.en: A person prepares to pump gas at a Valero gas station on June 16, 2026, in Austin, Texas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026年6月16日，一个人准备在德克萨斯州奥斯汀的瓦莱罗加油站加油。

contentParagraphs.en: Inflation slowed sharply in June as gas prices dropped — but it's a reprieve that may prove temporary as tensions with Iran once again ratchet up.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着天然气价格下跌，通货膨胀在6月份大幅放缓，但这可能是暂时的，因为与伊朗的紧张局势再次加剧。

contentParagraphs.en: Consumer prices in June were up 3.5% from a year earlier, the Labor Department said on Tuesday. That's down sharply from the annual increase of 4.2% seen in May, which had been the highest in over three years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 劳工部周二表示， 6月份的消费者价格同比上涨3.5%。这比5月份4.2%的年增长率大幅下降，这是三年来的最高水平。

contentParagraphs.en: The main factor slowing down inflation was a sharp drop in energy prices, which helped overall prices fall 0.4% in June compared to the 0.5% increase seen in May, according to the consumer price index (CPI) data.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据消费者价格指数（ CPI ）数据，通货膨胀放缓的主要因素是能源价格大幅下跌，这导致6月份整体价格下跌0.4% ，而5月份上涨0.5%。

contentParagraphs.en: The price at the pump for a gallon of regular gas at the end of June was 71 cents lower than they were during the May peak and have remained steady since, according to AAA. Gas prices fell in June after the U.S. and Iran agreed on a tentative cease-fire.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据AAA的数据， 6月底一加仑普通天然气的泵价格比5月高峰期低71美分，此后一直保持稳定。在美国和伊朗同意暂时停火后， 6月份天然气价格下跌。

contentParagraphs.en: But crude oil prices have been climbing back up as the ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. ended this month, Iran claimed the Strait of Hormuz was once again closed and the U.S. military announced it will reinstate its blockade of Iranian ships trying to pass through the strait. President Trump also said Monday that he would impose a 20% toll on all cargo going through the strait. All this led to fears that energy prices will spike again.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但随着伊朗和美国之间的停火于本月结束，原油价格一直在回升，伊朗声称霍尔木兹海峡再次关闭，美国军方宣布将恢复对试图通过该海峡的伊朗船只的封锁。特朗普总统周一还表示，他将对通过海峡的所有货物征收20%的通行费。所有这些都引发了人们对能源价格将再次飙升的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: "We're probably maybe a week away from seeing the national average again hitting $4.00," said Patrick De Haan, an analyst at GasBuddy. "The CPI party from June it's going to be crashed here I think for the month of July."
contentParagraphs.zh: GasBuddy分析师Patrick De Haan表示： “我们可能还需要一周的时间才能看到全国平均水平再次达到4.00 $。” “我认为6月份的CPI派对将在7月份在这里崩溃。”

contentParagraphs.en: Strip away food and energy prices and so-called "core" inflation for the 12 months ending in June was 2.6%, lower than in May.
contentParagraphs.zh: 去除6月份结束的12个月的粮食和能源价格以及所谓的“核心”通胀率为2.6% ，低于5月份。

contentParagraphs.en: The data comes as the Federal Reserve's new chair, Kevin Warsh, begins the first of two-days of testimony before Congress on Tuesday as investors look for signs of whether the central bank will have to raise interest rates before the end of year.
contentParagraphs.zh: 数据公布之际，美联储新任主席凯文·沃什(Kevin Warsh)将于周二在国会开始为期两天的证词，因为投资者正在寻找美联储是否必须在年底前加息的迹象。

articles.title: Environmental groups sue government to stop a big change to the Endangered Species Act
articles.summary: By altering the definition of the word "harm" as used by the Endangered Species Act, the Trump administration may limit how wildlife is protected in the United States. Environmental groups are suing.
articles.titleZh: 环保组织起诉政府阻止对《濒危物种法》进行重大修改
articles.summaryZh: 通过改变《濒危物种法》中“伤害”一词的定义，特朗普政府可能会限制美国保护野生动物的方式。环保组织正在提起诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: A Red-cockaded Woodpecker hovers momentarily before landing at its nesting cavity inside the trunk of a longleaf pine. This species is listed as threatened under the Endangered Species Act.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一只红冠啄木鸟暂时盘旋，然后落在长叶松树干内的筑巢洞。根据《濒危物种法》，该物种被列为受威胁物种。

contentParagraphs.en: By altering the interpretation of a single word in the Endangered Species Act, the Trump administration has made what could be a sweeping change to how wildlife is protected in the United States.
contentParagraphs.zh: 通过改变“濒危物种法”中单个词语的解释，特朗普政府对美国野生动物的保护方式进行了彻底的改变。

contentParagraphs.en: That word is "harm." For more than 50 years, the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service defined harm to refer to anything that injures or kills a protected organism, including "significant habitat modification or degradation" that might impact a species' ability to feed, reproduce or seek shelter.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这个词是“伤害”。“50多年来，美国鱼类和野生动物管理局将危害定义为伤害或杀死受保护生物的任何事物，包括可能影响物种进食、繁殖或寻求庇护的能力的“重大栖息地改变或退化”。

contentParagraphs.en: But now, the federal government has officially rescinded that definition — a move that is already being challenged in court.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但现在，联邦政府已正式撤销该定义—此举已经在法庭上受到质疑。

contentParagraphs.en: "This action restores common sense, respects private property, provides much-needed certainty for landowners and follows the statute Congress actually passed," Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum said in a statement Friday, when the change was announced.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这一行动恢复了常识，尊重私有财产，为土地所有者提供了急需的确定性，并遵循了国会实际通过的法规，”内政部长Doug Burgum在周五宣布这一变化时的一份声明中表示。

contentParagraphs.en: "The final rule will restore the definition of 'harm' to its original intent as written under the ESA, and will maintain protections for endangered species while reducing unnecessary or duplicative permitting requirements, cutting compliance costs, and eliminating confusion for Americans," a representative for NOAA Fisheries said in an emailed statement Tuesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: NOAA渔业代表周二在一份电子邮件声明中表示： “最终规则将把'危害'的定义恢复到ESA规定的原意，并将保持对濒危物种的保护，同时减少不必要或重复的许可要求，削减合规成本，并消除美国人的混淆。”

contentParagraphs.en: But environmental groups worry the rule change, which goes into effect on September 14, could destroy the wild places that endangered species depend on, undermining the protections that have helped sustain them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但环保组织担心， 9月14日生效的规则变更可能会破坏濒危物种赖以生存的野生地区，破坏有助于维持这些地区的保护措施。

contentParagraphs.en: Shortly after the new rule was published in the federal register Tuesday, the law firm Earthjustice, alongside more than half a dozen other environmental groups, filed a lawsuit in federal district court in Seattle against the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service and NOAA Fisheries (also known as the National Marine Fisheries Service).
contentParagraphs.zh: 新规则周二在联邦登记册上公布后不久， Earthjustice律师事务所与其他六个以上的环保组织一起，在西雅图联邦地区法院对美国鱼类和野生动物管理局和NOAA渔业（也称为国家海洋渔业局）提起诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: Kristen Boyles, an attorney with the group, told NPR that they're contesting the change on various levels. "The agencies haven't explained themselves adequately," she says. "Making this kind of dramatic change doesn't make any legal sense because it goes against the fundamental purpose and spirit of the statute itself. All of those things make it an unreasoned and unreasonable decision."
contentParagraphs.zh: 该组织的律师克里斯汀·博伊尔斯（ Kristen Boyles ）告诉NPR ，他们正在各个层面上对这一变化提出异议。“这些机构没有充分解释自己，”她说。“做出这种戏剧性的改变没有任何法律意义，因为它违背了法规本身的根本宗旨和精神。所有这些都使它成为一个不合理和不合理的决定。”

contentParagraphs.en: In the short term, Boyles anticipates that countless individual projects will be held up in court as these two understandings of the word "harm" — the historic definition and the administration's revised interpretation — duel for primacy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在短期内，博伊尔斯预计，由于对“伤害”一词的两种理解（历史性定义和政府的修订解释）争夺首要地位，无数个别项目将在法庭上被搁置。

contentParagraphs.en: "The one thing that this repeal of this rule is going to certainly do is cause complete confusion in the regulated community," says Boyles. "And it's going to cause an increase in litigation over each and every project that's proposed that doesn't protect habitat."
contentParagraphs.zh: 博伊尔斯说： “这项规则的废除肯定会在受监管的社区中造成完全的混乱。” “这将导致对每一个不保护栖息地的项目的诉讼增加。”

contentParagraphs.en: The Swinomish Indian Tribal Community and the Squaxin Island Tribe have also filed a lawsuit in U.S. District Court against the federal government. They explain that habitat degradation has been primarily responsible for the loss of salmon stocks in Puget Sound. Given that, they write in their complaint, "the loss of long-standing protection for the habitat of ESA-listed salmon species will injure the Tribes and their members."
contentParagraphs.zh: Swinomish印第安部落社区和Squaxin岛部落也在美国地方法院对联邦政府提起诉讼。他们解释说，栖息地退化是普吉特湾鲑鱼资源流失的主要原因。鉴于此，他们在投诉中写道， “失去对ESA上市鲑鱼物种栖息地的长期保护将伤害部落及其成员。”

contentParagraphs.en: Certain industry groups have weighed in to support the move. In public comments made last year when the rule change was initially proposed, a collection of oil and petroleum groups, including the American Petroleum Institute, wrote that the "proposal to rescind the 'harm' definition is justified many times over." And the Associated General Contractors of America wrote that they "appreciate the administration's efforts to reduce unnecessary regulatory burdens."
contentParagraphs.zh: 某些行业组织已经参与支持此举。在去年最初提出规则变更时发表的公开评论中，包括美国石油协会在内的一系列石油和石油集团写道， “废除'危害'定义的提议多次被证明是合理的。美国联合总承包商写道，他们“感谢政府为减少不必要的监管负担所做的努力。”

contentParagraphs.en: In a statement after the rule change was announced on Friday, Holly Hopkins of the American Petroleum Institute said, "We remain committed to supporting commonsense ESA [Endangered Species Act] policies that both protect wildlife and support American energy leadership."
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国石油协会（ American Petroleum Institute ）的霍莉·霍普金斯（ Holly Hopkins ）在周五宣布规则变更后的一份声明中表示： “我们仍然致力于支持常识性的ESA （濒危物种法案）政策，这些政策既保护野生动物，也支持美国的能源领导地位。”

contentParagraphs.en: The emergence of bold protections for species at risk
contentParagraphs.zh: 大胆保护濒危物种的出现

contentParagraphs.en: The Endangered Species Act has been a fixture of environmental regulation in the U.S. since the bipartisan bill was signed into law by President Richard Nixon in 1973.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自1973年理查德·尼克松总统签署两党法案成为法律以来，《濒危物种法案》一直是美国环境监管的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: At that time, says Holly Doremus, a professor of environmental law at UC Berkeley, Americans had grown worried that all kinds of wildlife, ranging from whales to wolves to whooping cranes, were in danger of being wiped out by commercial and development interests. The Endangered Species Act emerged that year as a kind of triage for species nearing extinction.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当时，加州大学伯克利分校环境法教授Holly Doremus表示，美国人越来越担心各种野生动物，从鲸鱼到狼再到百日咳起重机，都面临着被商业和发展利益消灭的危险。那一年，《濒危物种法案》（ Endangered Species Act ）出现了，作为对濒临灭绝物种的一种分类。

contentParagraphs.en: "Just as a patient would not be expected to spend the rest of their life in the emergency room," says Doremus, "species were supposed to be given special medical attention and treatment to recover."
contentParagraphs.zh: “就像患者不应该在急诊室度过余生一样，” Doremus说， “物种应该得到特殊的医疗照顾和治疗才能康复。”

contentParagraphs.en: In particular, the law prohibited the "taking" of members of an endangered species. The U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service delineated that the word "take" meant "to harass, harm, pursue, hunt, shoot, wound, kill, trap, capture, or collect." And "harm" was defined to include the alteration or destruction of critical habitat.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特别是，法律禁止“带走”濒危物种的成员。美国鱼类和野生动物管理局描述了“夺取”一词的意思是“骚扰、伤害、追捕、狩猎、射击、伤害、杀戮、陷阱、捕获或收集。“危害”被定义为包括重要栖息地的改变或破坏。

contentParagraphs.en: Doremus says the agency wasn't restricting the action of simply "shoot[ing] a critter. They wanted to be clear that if you, for example, removed the place where it nests, even if it was not there at the time, you would be hurting it."
contentParagraphs.zh: Doremus说，该机构并没有限制简单地“射杀小动物”的行动。他们想明确表示，例如，如果你移除它筑巢的地方，即使它当时不在那里，你也会伤害它。”

contentParagraphs.en: The thinking was that interfering with the place a protected species calls home would almost certainly be detrimental to the fate of that species.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当时的想法是，干扰受保护物种称之为家的地方几乎肯定会损害该物种的命运。

contentParagraphs.en: "For most all species, the number-one driver of extinction [is] damage and harm to their habitat, whether that's grazing, whether that's pollution, whether that's climate change," says Tara Zuardo, a senior campaigner at the Center for Biological Diversity, one of the organizations that's co-filing the lawsuit with Earthjustice.
